mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger household demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the trend is rising.
failure_condition: Reject if rising 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than falling RSAFS growth in at least 55% of walk-forward yearly test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.25% and stays there, very low discount rates support equity valuations and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% or remains below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a mean 60-day SPX return less than or equal to the unconditional mean return, or if the hit rate is below 50% in two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the labor market weakens materially.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with rising retail sales confirms that job growth is translating into spending, creating a reinforcing income-demand loop that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change exceeds +450 thousand and RSAFS 3-month percent change exceeds +1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS trigger fails to outperform either single-series trigger by at least 25 basis points in average 20-day forward return.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production alongside accelerating consumer prices signals margin compression and supply-side stress, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses below 0% while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have nonnegative average 20-day SPX returns or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus INDPRO alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and household confidence, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration during a near-zero policy-rate regime raises expected tightening risk and real-rate uncertainty, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation scares have average 5-day SPX returns above zero or if the signal has negative directional accuracy relative to the unconditional 5-day hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth captures broad demand and earnings expansion, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when growth momentum is above its recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 growth does not produce higher average 60-day SPX returns than below-trend GDPC1 growth across at least two consecutive walk-forward test windows.
